last_report: Slate of 11 games tonight; 2 picks drafted, both on SAS @ DEN. SAS L15 NetRtg dominance (+59.9 vs +29.1) drives strong edges on both ML (EV 0.27) and spread +2.0 (EV 0.33). Injury feed source is nba_official (full coverage) — all quality gates passed. Pace data not used (Pace = 0.0 universally suppressed). Most games rejected due to odds outside target range, tanking teams, or franchise player absences. PHI/DET game had no clean market: DET ML below 1.70, DET spread confidence falls below 60 after Cunningham OUT adjustment. CRITICAL commit flag: re-verify Victor Wembanyama active status via NBA official PDF before tip-off — if Wembanyama is OUT, reduce or void SAS picks per franchise player rules.

draft_picks.match: Denver Nuggets vs San Antonio Spurs
draft_picks.reasoning: SAS L15 NetRtg +59.9 vs DEN L15 +29.1 — gap of ~30.8 points, far exceeding the 4.0 threshold. Season NetRtg: SAS +8.4 vs DEN +4.8, a 3.6-point gap. SAS riding W11 streak, L10 10-0. DEN L10 8-2 but NetRtg divergence is stark at L15 level. No major injuries confirmed: DEN missing Spencer Jones, Zeke Nnaji, Peyton Watson (all non-franchise role players per skills file confirmation). SAS missing David Jones Garcia and Emanuel Miller (both non-franchise). Victor Wembanyama NOT in current verified absence feed — treating SAS as potentially full-strength. Odds check: SAS at 1.77 falls within ML target range (1.70–2.50). Anomaly check: SAS favoured on road — SAS is the higher-ranked team (59-18 vs 49-28), so pricing them at 1.77 road is slightly surprising but not anomalous given home court worth only ~0.10-0.15 odds adjustment. DEN is the home team at 2.07 which makes sense. No B2B for either team. Play-in/seeding: SAS locked into top-2 seed (59-18), rest risk possible but W11 streak suggests full effort. Late-season flag: star rest risk high for SAS — applying confidence -5 for this uncertainty. Base confidence 77, adjusted to 72 after rest risk penalty. EV = (0.72 × 1.77) - 1 = 1.2744 - 1 = 0.2744. EV = 0.27 — strong edge (≥ 0.15). Confidence 72 → High tier → stake 20-25% of bankroll. Using 20% = €57.67.

draft_picks.match: Denver Nuggets vs San Antonio Spurs
draft_picks.reasoning: Spread evaluation: DEN priced as -2.0 home favourite. SAS L15 NetRtg gap vs DEN is massive (+59.9 vs +29.1, diff ~30.8), well above the 6-point threshold for strong spread signal. Season NetRtg gap SAS +8.4 vs DEN +4.8 = 3.6 points. SAS getting +2.0 points as arguably the better team on current form is strong value. Spread odds 1.90 within target range (1.80–2.30). No B2B concerns. Injury feed: nba_official confirmed — full coverage, no gate restrictions. Advanced stats available — spread bet eligible. No game-time decisions for key players on either team in this game. Late-season rest risk for SAS (-5 confidence) applied, plus spread requires confidence ≥ 60 (met). Base confidence 75, adjusted -5 for rest risk = 70. EV = (0.70 × 1.90) - 1 = 1.33 - 1 = 0.33. EV = 0.33 — strong edge. NetRtg L15 gap > 6 points (it's ~30.8) confirms strong spread signal per skills criteria. Confidence 70 → High tier → stake 20% of bankroll = €57.67, but ML already placed on this game. Reducing spread stake to 15% = €43.25 to manage total exposure on single game.

settled_bets.match: Miami Heat vs Los Angeles Lakers
settled_bets.reasoning: Scout identified Miami Heat ML at 2.25 based on superior NetRtg (+3.1 vs Lakers +1.4), home court advantage, and Lakers' hot streak flagged as potentially luck-driven. At commit time, odds improved to 2.38 (+0.13 in our favour), consistent with news that Luka Doncic (Questionable, Right Hip) and LeBron James (Questionable, Left Foot Arthritis) are both uncertain for Lakers â€” two franchise-level players potentially unavailable. Austin Reaves also Questionable. Heat are missing Jaquez Jr. (confirmed OUT, was Questionable at Scout), Andrew Wiggins (OUT, new), and Terry Rozier (OUT, new), but their core rotation (Butler, Adebayo) remains intact. Net injury impact heavily favours Heat: Lakers potentially missing two franchise players outweighs Heat's role-player absences. Books have moved from 2.25 to 2.38, partially pricing in LAL risks, but 2.38 still offers strong value. EV at commit odds: (0.53 Ã— 2.38) - 1 = 0.2614 = 26.1%, well above 0.05 threshold. Odds within ML valid range (1.65â€“2.50). Confidence maintained at 53 (Speculative): LAL injury upside offset by Heat's additional absences. Stake: 10% of â‚¬1000 = â‚¬100. Original stake unchanged per rules (do not increase even on improved odds). Total exposure: 10% of bankroll.

settled_bets.reasoning: Celtics NetRtg +7.9 vs Grizzlies -2.9 = 10.8 pt gap (well above 6.0 threshold). Memphis confirmed tanking team (24-44, L10: 2-8) with catastrophic roster depletion confirmed at commit time: Aldama OUT, Caldwell-Pope OUT, Clarke OUT, Edey OUT, Gibson DOUBTFUL. GG Jackson no longer listed as Doubtful (potentially active), which explains the 1.5-point spread compression from -15.5 to -14.0. Boston (46-23, L10: 7-3) anchor players Tatum, Brown, Holiday all confirmed active â€” not on injury report. Spread moved from -15.5 to -14.0 (easier to cover, effectively line movement in our favor). Odds moved marginally from 1.91 to 1.90 (-0.01, within 0.05 drift allowance). EV = (0.80 Ã— 1.90) - 1 = 0.52. Confidence unchanged at 80 â€” Memphis depletion confirmed, Celtics core intact, strong motivation for seeding. Strong edge confirmed at commit time.

settled_bets.match: Denver Nuggets vs Portland Trail Blazers
settled_bets.reasoning: Portland arrives at Ball Arena catastrophically depleted: Damian Lillard (OUT - injury management), Shaedon Sharpe (OUT), Jerami Grant (upgraded from Questionable to OUT per NBA official injury report), and Vit Krejci (upgraded from Questionable to OUT). This is an even stronger edge than Scout projected. Denver (43-28, L10: 6-4) holds NetRtg +4.3 vs Portland -1.8 (gap 6.1 pts, above 4.0 threshold). Jokic active and anchoring Denver's offense. Peyton Watson confirmed active (not on injury report). Portland's offensive identity collapses without Lillard; with Grant also now OUT their veteran leadership and secondary scoring is removed. EV = (0.63 Ã— 1.94) - 1 = 0.222. Odds 1.94 within 1.80-2.30 target. Line moved slightly from -9.0 to -8.5 in current market (0.5pt tightening) but original pick at -9.0 at 1.94 remains valid and edge has strengthened. Confidence held at 63 (Scout's adjusted figure) given L15 unavailability.

Vit Krejci (POR â€“ OUT confirmed, upgraded from Questionable)

settled_bets.reasoning: Cleveland (45-27, NetRtg +4.3) hosts Miami (38-34, L10 5-5, NetRtg +2.5). Terry Rozier confirmed OUT (Not With Team). Jarrett Allen OUT for Cleveland already priced in. Critically, Scout's other Cleveland absences (Craig Porter Jr., Tyrese Proctor) do NOT appear on tonight's official injury report, suggesting those players are available — a positive upgrade for Cleveland (+5 confidence). Miami on a 4-game losing skid. Spread moved from -3.0 to -2.5 in our favour (easier line to cover). Cleveland's home court, better record, NetRtg advantage, and Miami's depleted guard rotation support covering 2.5 points. No B2B concerns for either team. Confidence 65 (up from 60 due to Cleveland roster upgrade). EV = (0.65 × 1.90) - 1 = 0.235 (23.5%). Odds at 1.90 within 1.80–2.30 spread target range.

settled_bets.match: Boston Celtics vs Oklahoma City Thunder
settled_bets.reasoning: OKC (57-15, L10 10-0, NetRtg +11.2 — best in the league) at Boston (47-24, L10 6-4, NetRtg +7.6). OKC road favourites priced at 1.79 (improved from Scout's 1.71 — movement in our favour). Jalen Williams confirmed AVAILABLE (not on tonight's official injury report). Boston missing Nikola Vucevic (OUT, fracture) — already priced in. OKC's only absentees are G League assignments (Barnhizer, Sandfort, Topic, Sorber). NetRtg gap of +3.6 pts in OKC's favour, with home court reducing effective gap to ~0.6–1.6 pts, but OKC's dominance (11.2 NetRtg, 11-game win streak) justifies road favourite pricing. Star rest risk minimal — road game at top-5 team (playoff seeding implications). Odds improved +0.08 at commit; confirming at original stake per line movement rules. EV = (0.68 × 1.79) - 1 = 0.217 (21.7%). Odds 1.79 within 1.70–2.50 ML target range.
anchor_players: Nikola Vucevic (Boston — OUT, Fracture, priced in)

settled_bets.match: Charlotte Hornets vs New York Knicks
settled_bets.reasoning: NYK ML confirmed at improved odds of 2.08 (Scout: 2.02, +0.06 in favour). All three anchor players (Brunson, KAT, Mikal Bridges) confirmed active per nba_official injury report. NYK record 48-25 (L10: 8-2) vs CHA 38-34 (L10: 7-3), season NetRtg +7.0 vs +4.8. Charlotte has new injuries vs Scout: Tidjane Salaun OUT (calf) and McNeeley OUT (illness), mildly reinforcing Knicks edge. Knicks in active East 2-3 seeding race — strong motivation positive. Injury source: nba_official (no confidence cap). EV at commit odds: (0.65 × 2.08) - 1 = 0.352. No anomaly detected. Stake held at original 15% of bankroll (€80.87). Odds in valid ML range [1.65–2.50]. Movement +0.06 in favour — stake not increased per line_movement_rules.

settled_bets.match: Oklahoma City Thunder vs Chicago Bulls
settled_bets.reasoning: OKC #1 seed (57-16, L10: 9-1) with elite NetRtg +10.9 vs Chicago's -4.4 — gap of 15.3 pts, well above 6pt spread threshold. SGA and Dort both confirmed active per NBA official injury report. Chet Holmgren listed OUT (hip) — not an anchor player; adjusted confidence from 72 to 67 (non-anchor significant player out). Chicago equally decimated: Collins OUT, Essengue OUT, Jaden Ivey OUT (new since Scout), Nick Richards Questionable — frontcourt gutted. OKC DefRtg 107.7 vs Chicago OffRtg 113.5; Chicago DefRtg 118.0 (porous). Odds improved marginally from 1.92 to 1.93 (in our favor). EV at confidence 67, odds 1.93: (0.67 × 1.93) - 1 = 0.293. Well above 0.05 threshold. Spread within valid range (1.75–2.30 floor at commit). Injury source: nba_official — no data quality restrictions apply. Stake: 15% of bankroll (medium confidence tier) = €458.24 × 0.15 = €68.74.

Detroit (53-20, L10: 8-2, NetRtg +7.8) is the materially superior team by record and efficiency vs Minnesota (45-28, L10: 5-5, NetRtg +3.6). Anthony Edwards (Minnesota franchise player) is confirmed OUT with patellofemoral pain, joined by Jaden McDaniels and Ayo Dosunmu. Cade Cunningham is also OUT for Detroit (pneumothorax), applying -10 confidence and -20% stake reduction per franchise_player_rules. Despite both teams missing key players, Detroit's underlying quality advantage (8-game record gap, +4.2 NetRtg differential, far superior L10 form) combined with Minnesota's triple-injury blow justifies confidence in Detroit at near-even odds. Detroit ML at 1.98 clears the odds floor (1.65) comfortably. EV = (0.65 × 1.98) - 1 = 0.287. Stake reduced 20% for Cunningham OUT.

LATE PICK — Franz Wagner status change since 14:00 Scout. Scout rejected Orlando ML because Wagner was listed OUT (key franchise-adjacent player). Current NBA official injury report does NOT list Wagner — he appears cleared and active. This is a material upgrade for Orlando. Meanwhile Toronto has THREE key players questionable: Brandon Ingram (Q-Inflammation), RJ Barrett (Q-Stiffness), Trayce Jackson-Davis (Q-Knee Tendinitis). Toronto (41-32, NetRtg +1.5) vs Orlando (39-34, NetRtg +0.8) — nearly identical records and ratings. With Toronto's depth at risk and Wagner likely active, Orlando at 2.04 offers genuine value. Line moved from 2.14 → 2.04 (-0.10) which is consistent with books partially adjusting for Wagner's availability, but hasn't fully priced in Toronto's multi-player questionable situation. Confidence build: Base 50 (even teams), Toronto home advantage offset (-5 for Orlando), Toronto 3 key players Q (+10), Wagner active upgrade (+10), Orlando's Isaac/Black OUT holes (-5) = 60. EV = (0.60 × 2.04) - 1 = +22.4%. Odds 2.04 in valid ML range (1.65-2.50). Injury source: nba_official (no cap restrictions). Time gate: 22:00 UTC tip, now 19:52 = 128 min > 20 min. Stake: Medium tier (55-69 confidence) = 15% × €435.31 = €65.30.

PHI NetRtg -0.2 with 41-33 record and L10 7-3 — classic hot streak with negative NetRtg fade candidate per skills. Hot streak fade rule: PHI W% ~.554 > .550 threshold with NetRtg -0.2 triggers explicit fade evaluation with confidence +10. Miami home (2-3pt advantage), NetRtg +2.2 positive. PHI injuries: Johni Broome Out (unchanged). MIA injuries: Norman Powell Out, Terry Rozier Out (unchanged). Anchor players verified: Joel Embiid active (not on injury report), Jimmy Butler active (not on injury report). Line moved only 0.02 against (2.08→2.06), within 0.10 threshold — no investigation needed. Odds 2.06 within ML target range 1.65-2.50. EV = (0.62 × 2.06) - 1 = 0.2772. Base confidence 52 + 10 hot streak fade = 62, Medium tier stake 15% of €370.01 = €55.50.

LATE PICK — new edge emerged since Scout. Boston severely depleted: Jayson Tatum OUT (Management, NBA official confirmed), Nikola Vucevic OUT (new — not in Scout data), Ron Harper Jr. OUT, Neemias Queta OUT — 4 players unavailable including franchise player. Atlanta has near-full strength (only G-League absences and Jock Landale illness). Atlanta home court advantage. ATL NetRtg +1.8 vs BOS NetRtg +7.8 — but BOS NetRtg calculated with healthy roster; with Tatum/Vucevic/Harper/Queta out, effective BOS quality is materially reduced. Jaylen Brown still leads Boston but lacks depth. Injury-dependent pick rule: Tatum confirmed OUT (not Questionable/GTD) — confirmation rule satisfied. EV = (0.60 × 1.88) - 1 = 0.128. Confidence 60 exactly at minimum gate. Medium tier stake 15% of €370.01 = €55.50. Time gate: tip 23:30 UTC, commit 22:27 UTC = 63 min > 20 min. Quantity gate: this is the 1 allowed late pick. Spread 1.88 within target range 1.80-2.30.

Late scout pick. Giannis Antetokounmpo confirmed OUT (hyperextension/bone bruise) via NBA official report — Milwaukee's franchise anchor absent. MIL also missing Thanasis Antetokounmpo, Gary Harris, Kyle Kuzma, Kevin Porter Jr., Bobby Portis — essentially their entire rotation. MIL record 30-45 (L10: 3-7), NetRtg -6.3 vs HOU NetRtg +4.7 (gap 11.0 points). HOU: 46-29, L10: 6-4, playing at home. HOU OffRtg 117.9 vs MIL DefRtg 118.9 — HOU should score at will. MIL OffRtg 112.6 with depleted roster means HOU defense (DefRtg 113.2) should limit them. Confidence: 65 base +10 Giannis OUT +5 mass MIL absences -10 extreme spread -18.5 +2 HOU form = 72. EV = (0.72 × 1.93) - 1 = 0.390. Line moved from -17.0 to -18.5 (1.5 pts against, consistent with Giannis OUT confirmation — books adjusting). Odds 1.93 within spread target range 1.80-2.30. Stake: 20% of €477.68 = €95.54 (High confidence tier).

settled_bets.match: LA Clippers vs San Antonio Spurs
settled_bets.reasoning: LATE PICK — Victor Wembanyama confirmed OUT (Management) for SAS after Scout phase. Books adjusted from 1.63 to 2.05, a 0.42 move explained entirely by Wembanyama's absence. At 2.05, SAS implied win probability is 48.8%. Assessment: SAS (58-18, L10 10-0, NetRtg +8.2) is a significantly superior team overall vs Clippers (39-37, L10 5-5, stats unavailable). Both teams missing anchor players (Wembanyama OUT for SAS, Beal OUT for Clippers). SAS's systemic depth, elite record, and winning culture offset the road disadvantage and absence of their franchise player. Confidence 60% that SAS wins outright — EV = (0.60 × 2.05) - 1 = 0.23. Staked at 15% of bankroll (medium-confidence tier floor) given confidence at the late-scout threshold.

Anthony Edwards was listed Questionable at Scout time (causing stake reduction to €15 and confidence 62), but the commit-time NBA Official injury report does NOT list Edwards — he is confirmed ACTIVE. Per injury_check_rules, anchor player upgraded from Questionable to Active → confidence +10 = 72. Stake recalculated at High tier (20% of €442.33 = €88.00). Odds improved from 2.14 (Scout) to 2.30 (+0.16 in our favor) — confirmed at original confidence level, not inflated. PHI season NetRtg -0.1 makes them a strong fade candidate despite 42-34 record. MIN is a credible playoff team (46-30, NetRtg +3.5) with Edwards now active. PHI injury report shows only Johni Broome OUT (non-anchor). EV = (0.72 × 2.30) - 1 = 0.656. Data source: nba_official (full coverage). No anomaly: MIN as away underdog at 2.30 is reasonable given PHI's mediocre underlying metrics. Total exposure remains well within 70% bankroll cap.

Late scout pick identified at commit phase. HOU is a legitimate playoff contender (47-29, L10: 6-4, L15 NetRtg: +35.9 — elite recent form, OffRtg 118.1, DefRtg 113.4) playing at home. UTA is confirmed tank tier (21-56, L10: 1-9, L15 NetRtg: -43.3) with a decimated roster: Collier OUT (hamstring), George OUT (strain), Jaren Jackson Jr. OUT (recovery), Walker Kessler OUT (recovery), Harkless OUT, Hinson OUT (G League) — essentially 6 contributors unavailable. This is one of the most extreme roster mismatches on tonight's slate. HOU still motivated for playoff seeding. UTA has almost no functional rotation. -18.0 spread is large but the L15 differential (+35.9 vs -43.3 = +79.2) is extraordinary. Odds 1.89 within valid spread range (1.75–2.30). EV = (0.62 × 1.89) - 1 = 0.172. Data source: nba_official. Late scout gates satisfied: first_game_time - now > 20 min, confidence ≥ 60, EV ≥ 0.05, within quantity limit (1 new pick). No pace data needed for spread pick. Total exposure €154.00 vs €309.63 cap.

Below ML floor of 50. CHA ML at 2.00: CHA has no L15 data available; no spread available. Insufficient data for clean edge on either side.

bets.reasoning: Anthony Edwards was listed Questionable at Scout time (causing stake reduction to €15 and confidence 62), but the commit-time NBA Official injury report does NOT list Edwards — he is confirmed ACTIVE. Per injury_check_rules, anchor player upgraded from Questionable to Active → confidence +10 = 72. Stake recalculated at High tier (20% of €442.33 = €88.00). Odds improved from 2.14 (Scout) to 2.30 (+0.16 in our favor) — confirmed at original confidence level, not inflated. PHI season NetRtg -0.1 makes them a strong fade candidate despite 42-34 record. MIN is a credible playoff team (46-30, NetRtg +3.5) with Edwards now active. PHI injury report shows only Johni Broome OUT (non-anchor). EV = (0.72 × 2.30) - 1 = 0.656. Data source: nba_official (full coverage). No anomaly: MIN as away underdog at 2.30 is reasonable given PHI's mediocre underlying metrics. Total exposure remains well within 70% bankroll cap.

Late scout pick identified at commit phase. HOU is a legitimate playoff contender (47-29, L10: 6-4, L15 NetRtg: +35.9 — elite recent form, OffRtg 118.1, DefRtg 113.4) playing at home. UTA is confirmed tank tier (21-56, L10: 1-9, L15 NetRtg: -43.3) with a decimated roster: Collier OUT (hamstring), George OUT (strain), Jaren Jackson Jr. OUT (recovery), Walker Kessler OUT (recovery), Harkless OUT, Hinson OUT (G League) — essentially 6 contributors unavailable. This is one of the most extreme roster mismatches on tonight's slate. HOU still motivated for playoff seeding. UTA has almost no functional rotation. -18.0 spread is large but the L15 differential (+35.9 vs -43.3 = +79.2) is extraordinary. Odds 1.89 within valid spread range (1.75–2.30). EV = (0.62 × 1.89) - 1 = 0.172. Data source: nba_official. Late scout gates satisfied: first_game_time - now > 20 min, confidence ≥ 60, EV ≥ 0.05, within quantity limit (1 new pick). No pace data needed for spread pick. Total exposure €154.00 vs €309.63 cap.

### CONFIRMED — Minnesota Timberwolves ML
- Match: Philadelphia 76ers vs Minnesota Timberwolves | Tip: 2026-04-03T23:00 UTC
- Pick: Minnesota Timberwolves ML | Scout odds: 2.14 | Commit odds: 2.30 | Move: **+0.16 (improved)**
- ML: H:1.63/A:2.30 | ATS: PHI -3.5 (1.90/1.92) | O/U: N/A
- Stake: €88.00 | Pot: €202.40 | Conf: 72/100 | EV: 0.656
- Decision: **UPGRADED AND CONFIRMED.** Scout flagged Anthony Edwards as Questionable, reducing confidence to 62 and stake to €15. Commit-time NBA Official injury report does NOT list Edwards — he is confirmed ACTIVE for tonight. Per injury upgrade rules, confidence increases by +10 to 72 (High tier). Stake recalculated at 20% of current bankroll (€442.33) = €88.00. Odds drifted favorably from 2.14 → 2.30 (+0.16), likely reflecting Edwards' confirmed availability being priced in differently elsewhere, but our edge at MIN ML is strengthened. PHI season NetRtg -0.1 remains a strong fade signal. PHI injury report shows only Johni Broome OUT (non-critical). EV = (0.72 × 2.30) - 1 = 0.656. Data source nba_official. This is a clean, high-confidence bet.

---

### CONFIRMED (LATE PICK) — Houston Rockets -18.0 Spread
- Match: Houston Rockets vs Utah Jazz | Tip: 2026-04-04T00:00 UTC
- Pick: Houston Rockets -18.0 Spread | Scout odds: N/A (new pick) | Commit odds: 1.89 | Move: N/A
- ML: H:1.06/A:10.0 | ATS: HOU -18.0 (1.89/1.93) | O/U: N/A
- Stake: €66.00 | Pot: €124.74 | Conf: 62/100 | EV: 0.172
- Decision: **ADDED AS LATE PICK.** Scout rejected HOU/UTA due to marginal EV and uncertainty. At commit time, UTA's depleted roster is now confirmed (6 players OUT including JJJ, Kessler, Collier, George) and the spread has moved to -18.0 with odds 1.89 (within valid range). HOU L15 NetRtg +35.9 is elite, they're home, motivated for playoff seeding at 47-29. L15 differential of +79.2 is the largest discrepancy on tonight's entire slate. UTA has almost no functional rotation. Confidence 62 (at gate minimum), EV 0.172 (well above 0.05). All late scout gates satisfied. This represents the strongest remaining edge on the slate after MIN ML.

---

### EXPOSURE CHECK
- Pick 1 (MIN ML): €88.00
- Pick 2 (HOU Spread): €66.00
- **Total committed: €154.00 / €442.33 bankroll = 34.8%** ✅ (well below 70% cap)

---

### SLATE SUMMARY
Tonight's primary edge is Minnesota Timberwolves ML at 2.30 — a clean play on PHI's poor underlying metrics (NetRtg -0.1) with Anthony Edwards now confirmed active, providing a significant upgrade from the Scout's cautious draft. The late-scouted Houston -18.0 spread is a secondary play capitalizing on Utah's historically depleted roster and Houston's elite recent form, representing one of the most extreme talent mismatches on the entire slate. Total exposure of €154.00 (34.8% of bankroll) is conservative and well within risk parameters. All other games on the slate lack either a viable odds range or sufficient confidence to warrant a bet, particularly given the prevalence of extreme favorites (1.05–1.12) and tanking teams creating unpredictable spread outcomes.
